title: Galipea febrifuga
intro: Galipea febrifuga is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, commonly known as "Palo de Fiebre" or "Fever Tree", which has been used in traditional medicine for its antimalarial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Galipea febrifuga include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of malaria, and treat various infections and diseases. The bioactive constituents of this plant include quinoline alkaloids, particularly galipeine and deoxypodophyllotoxin, which possess significant medicinal properties. Traditionally, Galipea febrifuga is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, malaria, and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Galipea febrifuga is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is a small, evergreen tree or shrub that belongs to the genus Galipea. Galipea febrifuga is classified as a member of the order Sapindales and the family Rutaceae, which includes citrus and rue species. The exact classification of Galipea febrifuga is as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: Angiosperms, clade: Eudicots, clade: Rosids, order: Sapindales, family: Rutaceae, genus: Galipea, species: G. febrifuga.
uses: Galipea febrifuga is a plant species that has been traditionally used in medicine for its antimalarial and anti-inflammatory properties. Its bark is a rich source of a compound called galipeine, which has been shown to have potential in treating malaria and other diseases. In traditional medicine, the plant is also used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. The antimalarial properties of Galipea febrifuga make it a valuable resource in regions where malaria is prevalent. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of this plant species.

properties: Galipea febrifuga, a plant native to the tropical forests of South America, is known for its traditional use in medicine, particularly for its antimalarial properties. The plant's roots and bark are used to extract compounds that have been shown to exhibit antiplasmodial activity, making it a potential candidate for the treatment of malaria. The active constituents of Galipea febrifuga also include alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal value.
